title: dianthus petraeus subsp. orbelicus
intro: Dianthus petraeus subsp. orbelicus, also known as Orbelian Pink or Orbelian Carnation, is a perennial plant species within the Dianthus genus, known for its fragrant pink flowers and medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dianthus petraeus subsp. orbelicus include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other remedies for various health conditions.
